description_long: This canopy features a pitched, gabled roof covered with dark gray shingles, supported by a timber frame structure. The base of each support column is encased in brickwork, providing both stability and a decorative touch. The wooden beams are arranged in a truss pattern, adding architectural interest and structural integrity. The open sides allow for easy passage beneath, while one end is partially enclosed with vertical wooden slats. The overall design is reminiscent of a traditional English lychgate or a park shelter, blending natural wood and masonry for a classic urban or suburban look. The canopy is robust and weather-resistant, suitable for protecting pedestrians or marking an entrance.
description: A pitched-roof canopy with timber framing and brick base supports, featuring open sides and partial wooden slat enclosure at one end.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open entrance, pitched roof, timber truss, and brick bases of the support columns.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full expanse of the shingled roof and the vertical wooden slats partially enclosing one end.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the open exit, pitched roof, and the symmetry of the timber and brick supports.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the shingled roof and the arrangement of vertical wooden slats at the end.
attribute_support_structure_design: The canopy is supported by four vertical timber columns, each set into a brick base for stability. The columns are connected by horizontal and diagonal wooden beams forming a truss, which supports the pitched roof. The design is open on two sides, with one end partially enclosed by evenly spaced vertical wooden slats.
attribute_edge_detailing: The roof edges are straight and clean, with exposed timber framing visible beneath the eaves. The brick bases have sharp, defined corners, and the wooden slats at one end provide a subtle decorative element.
